title: The Adventures of Roderick Random
author: Smollett, T. (Tobias)
summary: Tobias Smollett frames a picaresque tale in a prefatory argument for satirical fiction. He praises narratives that expose vice through plausible misfortune and names influences such as Cervantes and Le Sage. An apologue describes a lampooned conversation-piece—bear, owl, monkey, and ass—that escalates when offended patrons accuse the painter of personal satire. The narrator, Roderick Random, begins with his northern birth: son of his grandfather’s youngest, secretly married to a housekeeper. A prophetic highland seer foretells travel and dangers. Family discovery provokes grandfather’s displeasure; parental hopes, misunderstandings, and nascent misfortunes set the stage for the novel’s adventures and satirical exposure ensues.

characters.name: Roderick Random
characters.personality: modest, observant narrator; portrayed as genteel yet exposed to misfortune
characters.lifespan: born to a clandestine marriage; fate unresolved in the excerpt; story continues

characters.name: Grandfather (the old gentleman, judge)
characters.personality: stern, influential, legally minded, averse to beggars
characters.lifespan: alive in the opening; displeased by son's secret marriage

characters.name: Father (youngest son)
characters.lifespan: secretly married the housekeeper; remains dependent on his father

characters.name: Mother (the housekeeper)
characters.personality: devoted, anxious, impressionable (has a vivid dream)
characters.lifespan: gives birth to Roderick; her subsequent fate not given in excerpt

characters.name: Highland seer
characters.lifespan: interprets mother's dream and foretells the child's travels

characters.name: Officious person
characters.lifespan: informs the grandfather and provokes family disturbance

characters.name: The Devil (in the mother's dream)
characters.personality: fantastical, appears as a midwife in the dream
characters.lifespan: appears only in the dream; symbolic

characters.name: Young painter (apologue)
characters.lifespan: creates the conversation-piece and is accused of lampoon

characters.name: Bruin (bear in the painting)
characters.lifespan: part of the painted group used as an implied lampoon

characters.name: Owl (in the painting)
characters.lifespan: depicted and perceived by some viewers as a personal caricature

characters.name: Monkey (in the painting)
characters.lifespan: appears as the painter's figure in the picture

characters.name: Ass (in the painting)
characters.lifespan: depicted; patrons take offense thinking themselves represented

characters.name: Captain (offended patron)
characters.lifespan: confronts the painter angrily over the perceived lampoon

characters.name: Learned physician
characters.personality: erudite, vociferous, proud of medical learning
characters.lifespan: accuses the painter of depicting his features; objects loudly

characters.name: Venerable senator
characters.lifespan: claims the painting lampoons him and reproaches the painter

characters.name: Monsieur Le Sage
characters.lifespan: mentioned as a literary influence (Gil Blas)

characters.name: Cervantes
characters.lifespan: referenced in preface as reforming taste

characters.name: Xenophon
characters.lifespan: referenced via the Cyropaedia

characters.name: Gil Blas (character)
characters.lifespan: used as a model and point of comparison

characters.name: Aesculapius
characters.lifespan: referenced only in invective

characters.name: Christian reader
characters.lifespan: admonished to avoid taking offense

locations.name: Scotland (northern part of the United Kingdom / 'North Briton')
